mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> Ensure you are using the correct repository URL for the vLLM version.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes. This step ensures that all required CRDs are installed before deploying vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Ensure the namespace does not already exist or is empty.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example custom resource for vLLM, which defines the model and its configuration for serving.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace 'gpt-3' with your desired model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify vLLM Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment and ensures that the custom resource is ready for serving requests.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Look for the 'READY' status in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM custom resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command will remove the vLLM release.
> This command will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node where vLLM is running.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM custom resource is in a 'READY' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm.io/v1 is not a valid API version'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs are not installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified image does not exist or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens due to misconfiguration in the custom resource or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with kubectl logs <pod-name> and adjust the resource requests/limits accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service is taking too long to respond, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Scale up the number of replicas or increase resource limits in the custom resource.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new models and improved performance with enhanced batching algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to:
NAME              READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
example-vllm     2/2     Running   0          5m

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster is up and running with sufficient resources.
